mode_overview.title: DevOps Concert Shell V2 Mode - Complete Guide
mode_overview.introduction: The DevOps Concert Shell V2 mode is an enhanced version of the DevOps Concert Shell mode that adds intelligent false positive detection before proceeding with CVE remediation. This prevents unnecessary package upgrades and potential breaking changes by analyzing source code to determine if vulnerable package functions are actually used in exploitable ways.

feature.name: false_positive_detection
feature.description: Analyzes source code to detect false positive CVE alerts
feature.benefit: Prevents unnecessary package upgrades and breaking changes
feature.implementation: Uses search_files and read_file to analyze package usage

feature.name: intelligent_workflow
feature.description: Only creates PRs for true positive CVEs
feature.benefit: Reduces DevOps workload and CI/CD noise
feature.implementation: Comments on issue for false positives, creates PR for true positives

feature.name: concert_integration
feature.description: Integrates with Concert API for false positive reporting
feature.benefit: Keeps Concert dashboard accurate and up-to-date
feature.implementation: GitHub Actions workflow updates Concert after engineer confirmation

feature.name: detailed_analysis
feature.implementation: Detailed GitHub issue comments with evidence and reasoning

workflow_comparison.title: DevOps Concert Shell vs DevOps Concert Shell V2

original_workflow.name: devops-concert-shell

steps.step: 2. Research CVE in Concert and external sources

original_workflow.limitation: Always creates PR, even for false positives

enhanced_workflow.name: devops-concert-shell-v2

steps.step: 2. Research CVE in Concert and external sources
3. **NEW: Analyze source code for false positive detection**
4a. If FALSE POSITIVE: Comment on issue, tag engineer, exit
4b. If TRUE POSITIVE: Create remediation branch

enhanced_workflow.advantage: Prevents unnecessary PRs for false positives
Reduces breaking changes and CI/CD noise

actions.action: Parse GitHub issue for CVE details
Understand attack vector and exploitation requirements
Use search_files to find package imports
Identify all files using the package
Trace data flow to vulnerable functions
Check if user input reaches vulnerable code
Determine if vulnerable code paths are reachable

false_positive.criterion: Package is transitive dependency never used
Service not exposed to untrusted users

false_positive_action.action: Create detailed comment on GitHub issue
After approval: Update Concert API and close issue

true_positive_action.action: Create branch, update manifests, create PR

step.action: Detects false positive and comments on issue
Reviews analysis and approves devops-approval environment
Triggered by approval, calls Concert API

step.result: CVE marked as false positive in Concert dashboard

mode_configuration.description: Main mode configuration with roleDefinition and whenToUse

file.description: This file - complete mode overview and guide
Detailed false positive detection workflow and methodology
Complete workflow covering both false positive and true positive flows

example.name: github_actions_workflow
example.title: Using in GitHub Actions
example.workflow_yaml:
name: CVE Remediation with False Positive Detection
on: issues: types: [opened, labeled]
jobs: remediate-cve: if: contains(github.event.issue.labels.*.name, 'cve-remediation') runs-on: ubuntu-latest
steps: - name: Checkout uses: actions/checkout@v4
- name: Run Bob Shell V2 uses: ./.github/actions/bob-shell with: bobshell_api_key: ${{ secrets.BOBSHELL_API_KEY }} chat_mode: devops-concert-shell-v2 prompt: | Process GitHub issue #${{ github.event.issue.number }}.
Analyze the CVE for false positive detection before proceeding.
If false positive: Comment on issue with analysis. If true positive: Create remediation PR.

example.name: manual_invocation
example.title: Manual Invocation in Bob IDE
example.command: Switch to devops-concert-shell-v2 mode and run: "Process GitHub issue #123 with false positive detection"

scenario.name: transitive_dependency
scenario.description: Package is dependency of dependency, never directly used
scenario.detection: No direct imports found in codebase
scenario.example: App uses requests → urllib3 (CVE in urllib3 but never directly used)

scenario.name: unused_feature
scenario.description: Package used but vulnerable feature not used
scenario.detection: Package imported but vulnerable functions never called
scenario.example: Flask app uses routing but not sessions (CVE in session handling)

scenario.name: internal_only
scenario.description: Service internal-only, not exposed to untrusted users
scenario.example: Service on localhost/VPN, even if vulnerable code exists

scenario.name: mitigated
scenario.detection: WAF or validation before vulnerable code
scenario.example: Input validation blocks malicious input before vulnerable function

scenario.name: disabled_feature
scenario.example: XML parsing disabled even though library has CVE

best_practices.practice: Always perform false positive analysis before any package changes
Provide specific evidence with file paths and line numbers
When uncertain, default to TRUE POSITIVE (safer approach)
Include code snippets to support analysis
Consider deployment context and security controls
Document all findings clearly for engineer review

issue.name: search_fails
issue.solution: Document failure and default to TRUE POSITIVE

issue.name: inconclusive_analysis
issue.symptom: Cannot definitively determine if false positive
issue.solution: Default to TRUE POSITIVE and proceed with remediation

issue.name: complex_codebase
issue.symptom: Codebase too complex to analyze fully
issue.solution: Perform best-effort analysis, document limitations, default to TRUE POSITIVE

issue.name: concert_api_fails
issue.solution: Document issue, proceed with external CVE research, note Concert validation pending

migration_guide.title: Migrating from devops-concert-shell to devops-concert-shell-v2

step.action: False positive approval integrated into main workflow
step.change: Step 2A added to bob-shell-v2.yml for false positive approval
Add note about false positive detection capability
Educate DevOps engineers on reviewing false positive analyses

migration_guide.backward_compatibility: The original devops-concert-shell mode remains available for workflows that don't need false positive detection.

summary.title: Key Takeaways
summary.takeaway: DevOps Concert Shell V2 adds intelligent false positive detection to prevent unnecessary package upgrades and breaking changes.
The mode analyzes source code to determine if vulnerable functions are actually used in exploitable ways before creating remediation PRs.
False positives are reported via GitHub issue comments, with engineer confirmation triggering Concert API updates.
True positives proceed with normal CVE remediation workflow (branch, manifest updates, PR).
The mode reduces DevOps workload, CI/CD noise, and risk of breaking changes while maintaining security posture.
